paper_title: Trace-Level Analysis of Hexavalent Chromium in Lake Sediment Samples Using Ion Chromatography Tandem Mass Spectrometry

methods.description: This method describes the process of preparing and analyzing lake sediment samples for hexavalent chromium using ion chromatography coupled with tandem mass spectrometry (IC-MS/MS). The method is used to separate and detect chromium species in the samples.

steps.description: Prior to analysis, all extracts were diluted 10 to 5000-fold (v/v) with Milli-Q water and filtered using Mini-UniPrep™ syringeless filter devices.
Analysis was carried out using an ICS 5000 + ion chromatograph with specific settings for separation and detection.
The sample analysis was carried out on an API 3200 triple-quadrupole MS operated in negative ion mode using multiple reaction monitoring (MRM).
For data acquisition and handling, Analyst software version 1.6.1 and 1.6.2 and DCMS Link version 2.12 were applied.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire and handle data using software

methods.method_name: MPC-IC-MS/MS Analysis for Cr(VI) with Alkaline Digestion
methods.description: This method involves the analysis of Cr(VI) in lake sediment samples using a modified MPC approach with IC-MS/MS, avoiding traditional alkaline digestion to reduce interferences and improve accuracy.

steps.description: Avoid the addition of Mg(II) ions and a phosphate buffer to reduce interferences with the IC separation and MS/MS detection method.
steps.searchable_summary: Modify sample preparation to reduce interferences

steps.description: Use an internal standard to counteract matrix suppression/enhancement effects.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply internal standard for matrix effect correction

steps.description: Analyze 38 lake sediment samples using the IC-MS/MS method.

methods.description: This method uses speciated isotope dilution (SID) with IC-MS/MS and EDTA extraction to quantify Cr(VI) in lake sediment samples, minimizing oxidation and reduction interferences.

steps.description: Apply speciated isotope dilution approach for quantification of Cr(VI).
Perform alkaline EDTA extraction to reduce Cr(III) oxidation and precipitation.
Analyze extracts by SID-IC-MS/MS to obtain Cr(VI) concentrations.
Compare extraction protocols using microwave and ThermoMixer for efficiency.
